```{=typst}
#pagebreak()
#wcg-ws-open(
  day: "Day 6", num: 6, title: "Repetition tracker",
  purpose: "To keep the practice going lightly, without expecting perfection.",
  how: [Each day, simply mark whether you practiced. "Not yet" is a fine answer.
  There is no streak to protect and nothing to lose by missing a day.],
)

#let __trk-cb = box(baseline: 3.5pt, rect(width: 17pt, height: 17pt, stroke: 1pt + c-deep, radius: 2pt))
#let __trk-hd(s) = text(font: ("Manrope",), size: 9pt, weight: 700, tracking: 1.5pt, fill: c-num)[#s]
#let __trk-day(d) = text(font: ("Manrope",), size: 12pt, weight: 600, fill: c-deep)[#d]
#let __trk-line = box(width: 100%, inset: (bottom: 3pt), line(length: 100%, stroke: 0.6pt + c-taupe))

#block(above: 8pt, below: 4pt)[
  #grid(
    columns: (0.95in, 0.62in, 0.95in, 1fr), column-gutter: 16pt, row-gutter: 16pt,
    align: (left + horizon, center + horizon, center + horizon, left + horizon),
    __trk-hd("DAY"), __trk-hd("YES"), __trk-hd("NOT YET"), __trk-hd("ONE WORD ABOUT TODAY"),
    ..(("Day 1", "Day 2", "Day 3", "Day 4", "Day 5", "Day 6", "Day 7")
      .map(d => (__trk-day(d), __trk-cb, __trk-cb, __trk-line))
      .flatten())
  )
]

#wcg-group("My plan for a likely miss")
#wcg-field("A day that might be hard this week", lines: 1)
#wcg-field("What I will do to come back", lines: 1)

```

> **A gentle reminder.**
>
> In one real-world study, missing a single day did not stop a habit from forming.^11^
> One miss is just one day.

```{=typst}
#wcg-ws-footer()
```
